 {"id":"bd-22vun","title":"E2E Tests: ntm errors command with real tmux sessions","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end tests for the ntm errors command with real tmux sessions and error injection.

## Test Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_ntm_errors.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-ERRORS]"
TEST_SESSION="ntm_e2e_errors_$$"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

log() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX $(date +%H:%M:%S) $*"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; ((PASSED++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; ((FAILED++)); }

cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up test session: $TEST_SESSION"
    tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting ntm errors E2E tests"

# Setup: Create test session
log "Setup: Creating test session with multiple panes"
tmux new-session -d -s "$TEST_SESSION" -x 120 -y 30
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -v
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -h
sleep 0.5

# Inject errors into different panes
log "Injecting test errors into panes"

# Pane 0: Python error
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.0" 'echo "Traceback (most recent call last):" && echo "  File \"test.py\", line 1" && echo "FileNotFoundError: test.txt"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Pane 1: Go panic
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.1" 'echo "panic: runtime error: index out of range [5]" && echo "goroutine 1 [running]:"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Pane 2: Normal output
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.2" 'echo "INFO: Everything is fine"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Test 1: Capture all errors
log "Test 1: Capture all errors"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="$TEST_SESSION" 2>&1 || echo '{"errors":[]}')
log "Result: $RESULT"

ERROR_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.total_errors // .errors | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "Error count: $ERROR_COUNT"

if [[ "$ERROR_COUNT" -ge "2" ]]; then
    pass "Found errors in session"
else
    fail "Expected at least 2 errors, got $ERROR_COUNT"
fi

# Test 2: Filter by pane
log "Test 2: Filter by specific pane"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="$TEST_SESSION" --panes=0 2>&1 || echo '{"errors":[]}')
log "Result for pane 0: $RESULT"

HAS_PYTHON_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.errors[] | select(.line | contains("Traceback"))' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [[ -n "$HAS_PYTHON_ERROR" ]]; then
    pass "Found Python error in pane 0"
else
    fail "Missing Python error in pane 0"
fi

# Test 3: Check error structure
log "Test 3: Verify error structure"
FIRST_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.errors[0]' 2>/dev/null || echo "{}")
log "First error structure: $FIRST_ERROR"

HAS_PANE=$(echo "$FIRST_ERROR" | jq 'has("pane")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
HAS_LINE=$(echo "$FIRST_ERROR" | jq 'has("line")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")

if [[ "$HAS_PANE" == "true" ]] && [[ "$HAS_LINE" == "true" ]]; then
    pass "Error structure is correct"
else
    fail "Error structure missing fields"
fi

# Test 4: Non-existent session
log "Test 4: Non-existent session error handling"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="nonexistent_session_xyz" 2>&1 || echo '{"error":""}')
log "Result: $RESULT"

HAS_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq 'has("error")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
if [[ "$HAS_ERROR" == "true" ]]; then
    pass "Proper error for non-existent session"
else
    fail "Missing error for non-existent session"
fi

# Summary
log "=========================================="
log "E2E ntm errors Tests Complete"
log "Passed: $PASSED"
log "Failed: $FAILED"
log "=========================================="

[[ $FAILED -eq 0 ]] && exit 0 || exit 1
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test error capture from multiple panes
- [ ] Test pane filtering (--panes flag)
- [ ] Test error structure (pane, timestamp, line fields)
- [ ] Test non-existent session error handling
- [ ] Test Python traceback detection
- [ ] Test Go panic detection
- [ ] Proper cleanup on exit
- [ ] Detailed logging with [E2E-ERRORS] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:38:04.402775323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:02:27.598566315Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T07:02:27.598472669Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22vun","depends_on_id":"bd-38x1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-23ot","title":"Task: Implement conflict notification","description":"## Purpose
Notify users when file reservation conflicts are detected.

## Conflict Scenario
Agent cc_1 tries to edit file.go
But file.go is reserved by cod_1

## Notification Display
⚠️File Conflict Detected

myproject__cc_1 tried to edit: internal/cli/send.go
But it is reserved by: myproject__cod_2 (since 5 minutes ago)

Options:
  1. Wait for reservation to expire (25m remaining)
  2. Request handoff from myproject__cod_2
  3. Force-release (requires user confirmation)

[1] Wait  [2] Request  [3] Force  [Esc] Dismiss

## Integration Points
- Dashboard notification component
- Alert system for urgent conflicts
- Action handlers for each option

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conflict detected and displayed clearly
- Options presented to user
- Actions execute correctly
- Notification dismissible

## Technical Notes
Use existing notification patterns
Integrate with Agent Mail force_release_file_reservation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:38.073189767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:56:03.398967279Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:56:03.398150411Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23ot","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-23reb","title":"EPIC: WebSocket Streaming + Event Bus","description":"## Summary\nProvide a real‑time WebSocket layer for pane output, alerts, beads/mail/scanner updates, and other event streams with replay/resume support.\n\n## Scope\n- WebSocket hub, topic router, subscription protocol, event envelope.\n- tmux pipe‑pane streaming integration + fallback capture‑pane polling.\n- Event persistence with ring buffers + cursor resume.\n- Backpressure handling + per‑client throttling options.\n- Event adapters for system, mail, beads, scanner, pipelines, approvals, alerts.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Clients can subscribe to topics and resume from cursors.\n- Pane output streams reliably with low latency.\n- Backpressure does not crash server; dropped output is reported.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:48:27.018334109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:00:34.976027944Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-1kq4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-3oeov","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-cs5x4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-excs1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6s038","title":"TOON: Update documentation and examples","description":"Update README and other documentation for TOON support.

## Documentation Updates

- Add TOON output format to robot mode section
- Include examples with both JSON and TOON output
- Document performance benefits

### Example Updates
```bash
# JSON output (default)
ntm --robot-list-panes
{"panes":[{"name":"agent-1","program":"claude-code","active":true}]}

# TOON output (40-60% fewer tokens)
ntm --robot-list-panes --robot-output-format toon

```

 {"id":"bd-6wc0a","title":"TOON Integration: Token-Optimized Object Notation for Robot Mode","description":"Integrate TOON (toon_rust) to reduce token usage in robot mode output by 40-60%.

## Project Location
/dp/toon_rust (Rust library + CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Robot mode outputs JSON for every command (~60 commands). This JSON is:
- Read by AI agents (tokens cost money)
- Transmitted over network (bandwidth)
- Stored in logs (storage)

JSON is verbose with repeated keys, quotes, whitespace.

## What TOON Does

TOON is a token-efficient serialization format:
- 40-60% fewer tokens than equivalent JSON
- Designed specifically for LLM consumption
- Preserves type information
- Human-readable (unlike binary formats)
- Round-trip compatible with JSON

### Example
JSON: {"panes":[{"name":"agent-1","program":"claude-code","active":true}]}
TOON: panes:[{name:agent-1,program:claude-code,active:T}]

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### Option A: Output Format Flag (Recommended)
Add --robot-output-format flag: json (default) or toon (new)

### Option B: Config-Based Default
Set default output format in config: [robot] output_format = "toon"

### Option C: Auto-Detect
If agent supports TOON, use TOON automatically.

## Implementation Plan
1. Add toon_rust as Go dependency (via CGO/FFI or subprocess)
2. Create serialization wrapper that supports both JSON and TOON
3. Add --robot-output-format flag to all robot commands
4. Add config option for default format
5. Update documentation

## Considerations
- Backwards compatibility: existing scripts expect JSON
- Parsing: agents need TOON parser
- Errors: TOON errors should fall back to JSON
- caut integration: if caut parses robot output, it needs TOON support

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All child beads completed (bd-6s038, bd-1s6cs, bd-1zrjo, bd-1u462, bd-77bxi)
2. --robot-output-format flag works on all robot commands
3. TOON output produces valid TOON that can be decoded back to JSON
4. JSON output unchanged (backwards compatible)
5. 40-60% token reduction verified on sample outputs
6. Documentation updated with TOON examples

## Success Metrics
- Token usage reduced 40-60% for agents using TOON format
- No regression in JSON output
- Zero parsing errors in production","acceptance_criteria":"1. TOON format fully integrated
2. All robot commands support TOON output
3. 40-60 percent token reduction achieved
4. Backward compatible JSON default
5. Config and CLI options work
6. Comprehensive documentation
7. Unit tests for all components
8. E2E test for full TOON workflow","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:01:51.295366550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:14:19.401212517Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6wc0a","depends_on_id":"bd-1k3ga","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6z2bf","title":"Task: REST Agent Mail + reservations endpoints","description":"## Scope\nExpose Agent Mail MCP and file reservations via REST with full parity.\n\n## Endpoints\n- Mail: /mail/health, /mail/projects, /mail/agents, /mail/agents/create, /mail/agents/{name}, /mail/inbox, /mail/messages, /mail/messages/{id}/reply, /mail/messages/{id}/read, /mail/messages/{id}/ack, /mail/search, /mail/threads/{id}/summary, /mail/contacts/request, /mail/contacts/respond, /mail/contacts, /mail/contacts/policy.\n- Reservations: /reservations (POST/DELETE/GET), /reservations/{id}/release, /reservations/{id}/renew, /reservations/{id}/force-release, /reservations/conflicts.\n\n## Logging\n- Log agent names, thread IDs, reservation IDs, and conflicts.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: parameter validation + error mapping to MCP errors.\n- Integration: create project, send message, fetch inbox.\n- WebSocket: emit mail/reservation events on changes.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- REST endpoints map cleanly to MCP Agent Mail methods.\n- Reservation conflicts are surfaced with proper error codes.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:50:26.895729669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:11:39.756722520Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:11:39.756704496Z","close_reason":"REST endpoints for Agent Mail and file reservations fully implemented in internal/serve/mail.go","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6z2bf","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-71cpj","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"## Overview
Generate shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, and fish shells.

## User Story
As a user who frequently uses ntm, I want tab completion so that I can:
- Quickly complete command names (ntm sp[TAB] -> ntm spawn)
- Complete flag names (ntm spawn --c[TAB] -> --cc)
- Complete session names from running sessions
- Complete agent names/IDs for targeted operations

## Implementation
Using cobra's built-in completion generation:
- Add completion subcommand supporting bash, zsh, fish, powershell
- Register custom completion functions for dynamic values (sessions, agents)

## Dynamic Completions
- Session names: List active tmux sessions
- Agent IDs: List agents in current/specified session
- Bead IDs: List open beads for assign command
- Agent types: cc, cod, gmi

## CLI Usage
```bash
# Generate bash completion
ntm completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/ntm

# Generate zsh completion
ntm completion zsh > /usr/local/share/zsh/site-functions/_ntm

# Generate fish completion
ntm completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/ntm.fish
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ntm completion bash outputs valid bash completion script
- [ ] ntm completion zsh outputs valid zsh completion script
- [ ] ntm completion fish outputs valid fish completion script
- [ ] Session names complete dynamically
- [ ] Agent IDs complete dynamically
- [ ] Flag names and values complete correctly
- [ ] Documentation in help output","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:01:08.374795401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:01:29.705348315Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-71cpj","depends_on_id":"bd-22nh8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-75iqr","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-spawn for creating ensembles","description":"Implement --robot-ensemble-spawn for creating ensembles.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION \
  --preset=architecture-review \
  --question='Review auth system' \
  --agents=cc=3,cod=2

# Explicit custom modes (advanced requires opt-in)
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION \
  --modes=A1,B13,F43 \
  --allow-advanced=true \
  --question='...'
```

### Modifiers (unprefixed)
- `--preset` or `--modes` (one required)
- `--question` (required)
- `--agents` (cc/cod/gmi counts)
- `--assignment` (round-robin|affinity|explicit)
- `--allow-advanced`
- `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`
- `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`
- `--project` (override working dir)

- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per robot-api-design.md.
- Accept mode IDs or codes in `--modes` (normalize internally).

## Response Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "action": "ensemble_spawn",
  "session": "arch-review-2024-01-15",
  "preset": "architecture-review",
  "question": "Review authentication system architecture",
  "modes": [
    {"id":"deductive","code":"A1","tier":"core","pane":"..."},
    {"id":"abductive","code":"B13","tier":"advanced","pane":"..."}
  ],
  "budget": {"total_tokens": 30000},
  "status": "spawning"
}
```

## Validation Rules
- Reject advanced/experimental modes unless `allow_advanced=true`
- Reject unknown mode ids/codes
- Enforce min/max mode count
- Error codes use standard robot codes where possible:
  - `MISSING_REQUIRED` (question or preset/modes)
  - `INVALID_FLAG` (preset + modes together, incompatible flags)
  - `INVALID_INPUT` (unknown mode id/code, invalid agents)

## File Location
internal/robot/ensemble_spawn.go

## Dependencies
- EnsembleManager (bd-lp5h4)
- Validation (bd-21ygt)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawns ensemble with preset or explicit modes
- [ ] Honors allow_advanced gating
- [ ] RobotResponse envelope compliance
- [ ] Clear error codes for invalid inputs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:54.697779752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:43:15.965813817Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T20:43:15.965793990Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-75iqr","depends_on_id":"bd-21ygt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-75iqr","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-765vk","title":"Bug: truncate() should handle maxLen <= 0","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:18:59.348464928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:19:10.819397696Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:19:10.819333136Z","close_reason":"Fixed truncate() edge cases + added unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-77bxi","title":"TOON: Research Go integration approach (CGO vs subprocess)","description":"Evaluate approaches for calling toon_rust from Go code.

## Options

### Option A: Subprocess (Simpler)
Call toon CLI as subprocess:
```go
cmd := exec.Command("toon", "encode")
cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(jsonData)
toonOutput, _ := cmd.Output()
```

Pros: Simple, no CGO, easy updates
Cons: Process overhead per call, latency

### Option B: CGO + Rust FFI (Faster)
Build toon_rust as C-compatible library, call from Go via CGO.

Pros: Near-zero overhead, native speed
Cons: CGO complexity, cross-platform builds, version coupling

### Option C: WASM (Middle Ground)
Compile toon_rust to WASM, run via Go WASM runtime.

Pros: Sandboxed, cross-platform, single binary
Cons: WASM overhead, runtime dependency

## Recommendation

Start with **subprocess** for simplicity. If latency becomes issue, migrate to CGO.

Robot mode commands are not high-frequency (maybe 1-10/second), so subprocess overhead (~5ms) is acceptable.

## Research Tasks
1. Verify toon CLI exists and has encode/decode commands
2. Benchmark subprocess overhead
3. Evaluate if CGO is needed for any use case
4. Check if toon_rust has WASM build
